title: malus rivularis
intro: Malus rivularis, also known as the river crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family native to China. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus rivularis are said to include reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating diarrhea, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be consumed as a fruit or used in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
